automotive-ev-battery.title: CATL Developing 12,000 Wh Per Kg Lithium-Air Battery
automotive-ev-battery.summary: CATL announced development of lithium-air batteries with potential energy density of up to 12,000 Wh/kg, representing a significant breakthrough in next-generation battery technology. This advancement could substantially extend electric vehicle range and improve overall EV performance beyond current lithium-ion capabilities.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD plans to install its Flash Chargers at Sinopec petrol stations
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD has partnered with Sinopec to deploy its Flash Charging fast-charging network across China's over 30,000 petrol stations, leveraging existing infrastructure to accelerate EV charging expansion. This strategic collaboration combines BYD's charging technology with Sinopec's nationwide distribution footprint.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD's 'world's first' 1,000-km range luxury GT is off to a hot start
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD's new Denza Z9 GT luxury sedan offers over 1,000 km of range, sub-10-minute recharging, and starts at approximately $40,000 in China, now expanding to European and additional international markets. The vehicle represents a significant achievement in long-range EV capability at competitive pricing.

automotive-ev-battery.title: EVs Take 33.0% Share In France — Tesla Model Y Leads
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Battery electric vehicles achieved a 33.0% market share in France during Q1 2026, up significantly from 22.9% in the prior year, with the Tesla Model Y leading sales. The strong growth demonstrates accelerating EV adoption in Western Europe despite overall auto volume declining 2.5% year-over-year.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Volkswagen's push for more affordable EVs starts with these two
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen has begun production of the ID. Polo and Cupra Raval affordable mass-market electric vehicles at its Martorell plant in Spain, marking the start of the automaker's strategy to expand accessible EV offerings. These models represent the first of several new affordable vehicles planned by the Volkswagen Group.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Malaysia: Stellantis starts local assembly of Leapmotor C10
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Stellantis has commenced local assembly of the Leapmotor C10 mid-size SUV in Malaysia, establishing its first locally built EV model in the ASEAN region. The move strengthens Stellantis' competitive position and supply chain presence in Southeast Asia's growing electric vehicle market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: GM Pitches New LMR EV Battery On Earth, New EV On The Moon
automotive-ev-battery.summary: General Motors is developing lower-cost LMR battery technology for consumer electric vehicles while participating in the Pegasus lunar rover program with NASA. The dual focus demonstrates GM's commitment to advancing EV affordability while exploring aerospace applications of battery technology.

automotive-ev-battery.title: IONCOR Energy Pack: Compact Power for Heavy-Duty Electrification
automotive-ev-battery.summary: IONCOR has launched its Energy Pack, a compact, high-performance battery platform designed for heavy-duty vehicle and off-highway equipment electrification where space constraints and reliability are critical. The off-the-shelf solution addresses safety, packaging, certification, and lifecycle cost challenges in heavy-duty EV applications.

automotive-ev-battery.title: PHEV Sales Continue to Record Phenomenal Growth in South Africa
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Plug-in hybrid vehicle sales in South Africa surged 280% year-over-year in 2025, becoming the fastest-growing powertrain segment in the market. The exceptional growth reflects changing consumer preferences and increasing availability of PHEV models in the region.

automotive-ev-battery.title: When public charging stations aren't so public, and why it matters
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Many so-called public EV charging stations at car dealerships are effectively restricted by customer-only policies, store hours, security gates, and inconsistent pricing, undermining the charging experience for first-time EV drivers. The lack of truly open public charging infrastructure creates negative perceptions of both the charging network and electric vehicles themselves.

tech-ai.title: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI has introduced Lockdown Mode, a security feature designed to reduce the risk of sensitive data exposure through prompt injection attacks on ChatGPT. While the new mode does not completely eliminate vulnerability to such attacks, it aims to significantly lower the likelihood of unauthorized data sharing during conversations.

tech-ai.title: The Trump administration might take an equity stake in OpenAI
tech-ai.summary: President Donald Trump has indicated ongoing discussions about potential government deals involving OpenAI that would allow the American public to benefit from AI sector success. The arrangement could involve the Trump administration acquiring an equity stake in the company as part of broader artificial intelligence policy initiatives.

tech-ai.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
tech-ai.summary: Security researchers have discovered that a USB-connected speaker can be remotely compromised to infect a personal computer through over-the-air hacking, despite the manufacturer's assertion that the behavior does not constitute a vulnerability. The vulnerability raises concerns about the security of consumer hardware devices connected to computing systems.

tech-ai.title: Sriram Krishnan is leaving his role as White House AI advisor
tech-ai.summary: Sriram Krishnan has departed from his position as a White House AI advisor and is reportedly establishing a new institution to continue influencing Trump administration artificial intelligence policy. His departure marks a significant personnel change in the federal government's AI policy leadership.

tech-ai.title: Meta made its own AI-generated clickbait news feed
tech-ai.summary: Meta's standalone AI application now features a "For You" section populated with AI-generated clickbait-style news stories, complete with algorithmically created topics, images, and text. The development represents Meta's foray into automated content generation for news feeds, though the quality of AI-generated material remains questionable.

tech-ai.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri's highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
tech-ai.summary: Apple's Worldwide Developers Conference 2026 is approaching with expected announcements regarding a major overhaul of Siri and advancements in Apple Intelligence capabilities. The conference will likely showcase the company's latest artificial intelligence and software innovations.

tech-ai.title: Beyond Instagram: Introducing the next generation of social apps
tech-ai.summary: A new wave of social applications is emerging as alternatives to Big Tech's dominant platforms, emphasizing user interests, creative expression, and community engagement over traditional algorithmic feeds. These emerging applications represent a shift in how developers are approaching social networking architecture.

tech-ai.title: Benn Jordan longs for the days of tech that didn't spy on you
tech-ai.summary: Content creator Benn Jordan has shifted his YouTube channel from music gear reviews toward science and technology investigations, increasingly focusing on privacy and surveillance issues in consumer technology. Jordan has expanded his work into a nonprofit organization dedicated to examining how modern devices monitor users.

tech-ai.title: GOG apologizes for emailing people Nazi symbols
tech-ai.summary: Good Old Games (GOG) issued an apology after its newsletter promoting the fantasy game The End of the Sun inadvertently included symbols associated with the Nazi SS. The company attributed the error to a combination of miscommunication with German QA staff, font rendering issues, and staffing shortages during a bank holiday.

tech-ai.title: The mayor of Shelbyville, Indiana, says only people who live in 'shitty houses' oppose data center
tech-ai.summary: A proposed $2 billion data center project in Shelbyville, Indiana has become a contentious political issue, intensified by remarks from Mayor Scott Furgeson dismissing opposition signs as appearing predominantly on lower-income properties. The controversy highlights broader tensions between economic development initiatives and community concerns in small American towns.

finance-markets.title: ECB Steps Up as G7's Lead Hawk With Interest-Rate Hike Primed
finance-markets.summary: The European Central Bank is positioned to lead global monetary tightening with an imminent interest-rate hike, driven by inflationary pressures stemming from the Iran war. This move marks the ECB's emergence as the most hawkish central bank among G7 nations amid geopolitical disruptions.

finance-markets.title: Fed's Barr Warns of Risks Tied to Looser Wall Street Bank Rules
finance-markets.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr has cautioned that recent regulatory rollbacks for US lenders represent a significant weakening of banking oversight and supervision. His warnings highlight growing concerns about financial stability as banking regulations face proposed loosening.

finance-markets.title: Trump relaunches tariffs war after court struck down levies
finance-markets.summary: President Trump is pursuing a new framework of punitive trade measures designed to withstand legal scrutiny after courts invalidated previous tariffs. The administration is attempting to craft legally defensible protectionist policies amid ongoing trade tensions.

finance-markets.title: Bouygues Telecom consortium agrees to buy Patrick Drahi's SFR for €20.35bn
finance-markets.summary: A consortium including Orange and Free-Iliad has agreed to acquire SFR for €20.35 billion, marking a significant consolidation in European telecommunications. The deal faces critical regulatory review from antitrust authorities in both France and the European Union.

finance-markets.title: Oil tanker owners fear market crash after Iran war drove record profits
finance-markets.summary: Shipping companies capitalised on wartime disruptions to achieve record profitability but face severe downside risk if tensions ease and the Strait of Hormuz reopens. Many operators have committed windfall gains to new vessel construction, exposing them to margin compression.

finance-markets.title: Chinese EVs may hit U.S. within a few years, one way or another
finance-markets.summary: Chinese electric vehicle manufacturers are poised to enter the US market within years despite regulatory barriers, tariffs and political opposition from domestic industry stakeholders. Industry analysts suggest tariff protections may prove insufficient to prevent Chinese automotive competition.

finance-markets.title: DoubleLine, Oaktree Brace for Potential AI Pain
finance-markets.summary: Major credit managers DoubleLine Capital and Oaktree Capital are strategically positioning their portfolios with defensive debt securities to protect against potential credit market disruption from an artificial intelligence downturn. The moves reflect growing concern that the AI boom may be unsustainable.

finance-markets.title: World's Hottest Market Has Korea Bulls Reaching for Protection
finance-markets.summary: South Korean equity markets are experiencing a sentiment shift as bullish investors increasingly hedge positions amid concerns that the market rally has accelerated excessively. Growing caution is replacing prior optimism as crowded trades unwind.

finance-markets.title: Tokio Marine boss to use Berkshire deal for global growth drive
finance-markets.summary: Tokio Marine's chief executive is leveraging a Berkshire Hathaway partnership to accelerate the Japanese insurer's international expansion, with ambitions to rank among the world's top five insurers within ten years. The strategic initiative capitalises on enhanced capital and operational capabilities.

finance-markets.title: What we know about the plan to give Americans an equity stake in AI
finance-markets.summary: OpenAI has proposed establishing a sovereign-wealth-style fund that would grant American citizens equity participation in artificial intelligence development and returns. The initiative aims to address public concerns regarding AI's societal impact and wealth concentration.

science-space.title: Going supersonic! NASA's X-59 jet breaks sound barrier for the 1st time
science-space.summary: NASA's X-59 experimental jet successfully broke the sound barrier for the first time on June 5, reaching a top speed of approximately Mach 1.1. This milestone represents a significant achievement in NASA's quiet supersonic technology research program.

science-space.title: A Moonlit Earth as Seen From Artemis II
science-space.summary: An astronaut aboard the Artemis II mission captured a striking photograph of Earth illuminated by moonlight during the spacecraft's journey to the Moon. The image provides a unique perspective on our planet's place in the cosmos.

science-space.title: Tiny X-ray telescope could unlock the Moon's hidden chemistry
science-space.summary: Researchers have developed a compact X-ray telescope capable of producing a complete chemical map of the Moon's surface from lunar orbit. The breakthrough could reveal key insights into the Moon's formation and geological evolution.

science-space.title: Hidden supermassive black hole pairs may finally have a visible signal
science-space.summary: Scientists have proposed a novel detection method for closely orbiting supermassive black hole pairs by identifying repeating stellar flash patterns created by gravitational lensing. These distinctive brightness signatures could help astronomers locate and study black holes in the final stages before collision.

science-space.title: Scientists found a surprisingly simple way to create powerful quantum states
science-space.summary: Researchers at the University of Chicago have developed a simple method for generating highly entangled quantum states by making minor adjustments to atomic energy levels within an optical cavity. The technique eliminates the need for complex additional hardware while producing powerful quantum systems.

science-space.title: First Steps: America's Grueling Second Spacewalk
science-space.summary: One year after Edward H. White's historic first NASA spacewalk, astronaut Gene Cernan prepared for a demanding second extravehicular activity aboard Gemini IX-A on June 5, 1966. The mission highlighted the physical and technical challenges of conducting spacewalks during the early space program.

science-space.title: Giant fire tornadoes could clean up oil spills faster with less pollution
science-space.summary: Researchers have demonstrated that controlled fire whirls can remediate oil spills more efficiently and cleanly than traditional burning methods, consuming up to 95 percent of oil while reducing soot emissions by 40 percent. The technique could provide a valuable tool for protecting sensitive marine environments from spill contamination.

science-space.title: A tiny atomic shift gives scientists powerful control over metals
science-space.summary: University of Minnesota researchers discovered that adjusting metal film thickness by just a few nanometers dramatically alters its electronic behavior. The finding opens new possibilities for advancing electronics, catalysis, and quantum technology applications.

science-space.title: Fighting Fire With Fire
science-space.summary: NASA researchers document how prescribed burns in Australia's Northern Territory reduce the severity of uncontrolled wildfires later in the fire season. The technique demonstrates effective ecosystem management in fire-prone regions.

science-space.title: Scientists sound the alarm as dangerous amoebas spread globally
science-space.summary: Scientists warn that free-living amoebae represent an underappreciated public health threat capable of causing deadly infections and protecting other pathogens from water treatment processes. Climate change and deteriorating water infrastructure could accelerate the global spread of these resilient organisms.

world-news.title: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf in latest test of ceasefire
world-news.summary: The United States military conducted strikes against Iranian drones and radar installations, while Iran responded with attacks on US military bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. The escalating exchange represents a critical test of ceasefire efforts in the region amid heightened US-Iran tensions.

world-news.title: Ukrainian drones target St Petersburg in attack Russia calls 'unprecedented'
world-news.summary: Ukraine conducted a significant drone attack on Russia's second-largest city, St Petersburg, prompting Russian authorities to order residents to shelter indoors for the first time since the war began. The strike marks an escalation in Ukraine's direct attacks on Russian territory.

world-news.title: Pentagon said to raise threat level on Israel spying to 'critical'
world-news.summary: The US Department of Defense has elevated its threat assessment regarding Israeli espionage activities to critical levels amid ongoing Middle East hostilities and ceasefire negotiations. The escalation reflects growing concerns about intelligence collection within the US-Israeli military partnership.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Israel kills Lebanon general, Pakistan urges end to war
world-news.summary: Israeli forces killed three senior Lebanese military officers including a general in southern Lebanon, as Pakistan and other Arab nations have called for an end to regional hostilities. The incident intensifies ongoing conflict between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon.

world-news.title: Lebanese general among three soldiers killed in Israeli attack on car
world-news.summary: An Israeli military operation in southern Lebanon killed three soldiers including a high-ranking general, with the military conducting an investigation into the incident. The strike occurred as Israel continues military operations against Hezbollah in the region.

world-news.title: US doctor recovers from Ebola in Germany as DRC cases surge to 488
world-news.summary: A US physician recovered from Ebola infection at Berlin's Charite hospital, marking a significant therapeutic success, while the Democratic Republic of Congo continues facing a growing outbreak with confirmed cases exceeding 488. The case highlights both medical advances in treatment and ongoing public health challenges in affected regions.

world-news.title: Hegseth attacks Europe over migration with beach 'invasion' D-Day speech
world-news.summary: US Defense Secretary Hegseth criticized European nations' migration policies during a speech at Normandy on the 82nd anniversary of D-Day, drawing controversy for his rhetoric. The comments reflect broader transatlantic tensions over immigration policy and defense priorities.

world-news.title: Canada bans Texas cattle over flesh-eating screwworm outbreak in US
world-news.summary: Canada has implemented a cattle import ban from Texas following the identification of a second infected animal carrying the flesh-eating screwworm parasite. Texas has declared a state of disaster as the outbreak threatens livestock across North America.

world-news.title: Rights group says drone strike kills 11 in central Sudan market
world-news.summary: An airstrike on a market in central Sudan killed at least 11 civilians with dozens more wounded, according to emergency legal advocacy groups documenting the incident. The strike follows similar aerial attacks within 24 hours as Sudan's humanitarian crisis deepens.

world-news.title: Iran says staff blocked from entering US after players given World Cup visas
world-news.summary: Iranian officials reported that technical staff have been denied US entry visas hours after the State Department authorized visas for Iranian athletes competing in an upcoming tournament. The diplomatic incident reflects ongoing US-Iran tensions affecting bilateral relations.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin, ether eye worst weekly rout since FTX collapse as cryptos shed $390 billion
crypto-web3.summary: The cryptocurrency market experienced one of its largest drawdowns in years this week, with digital assets losing approximately $390 billion in value. The decline marks the worst weekly performance for bitcoin and ether since the FTX collapse, signaling significant market stress and investor capitulation.

crypto-web3.title: America's largest banks are building a new digital currency network to stop a massive deposit drain
crypto-web3.summary: Major U.S. banks are launching tokenized deposit networks to compete directly with stablecoins and address customer deposit migrations. This development opens a new competitive front in establishing the dominant form of blockchain-based cash and represents traditional finance's direct response to crypto innovation.

crypto-web3.title: Next up, taxes: Crypto tax legislation circulated ahead of House Ways and Means Committee hearing next week
crypto-web3.summary: Cryptocurrency tax legislation is being prepared for a House Ways and Means Committee hearing as regulators move to establish comprehensive tax frameworks for digital assets. This regulatory development follows broader efforts to regulate stablecoins and the cryptocurrency market as a whole.

crypto-web3.title: Meta is paying creators in Stablecoins. Spending them is someone else's problem
crypto-web3.summary: Meta's adoption of USDC for creator payments validates stablecoins as a mainstream disbursement tool but highlights a critical gap in the industry: the inability to seamlessly convert digital dollars into usable local currency. The move accelerates stablecoin adoption while exposing infrastructure deficiencies in the ecosystem.

crypto-web3.title: Wallet linked to Ethereum co-founder Joseph Lubin moves 110,000 ETH to defend $259 million DAI debt position
crypto-web3.summary: A wallet associated with Ethereum co-founder Joseph Lubin transferred 110,000 ETH to manage a $259 million DAI collateral position amid market volatility. On-chain analysts characterized the move as defensive collateral management to mitigate liquidation risk rather than a token sale.

crypto-web3.title: Securitize clears SEC registration statement hurdle, sets path to NYSE listing as SECZ
crypto-web3.summary: Securitize has cleared a critical SEC registration milestone, advancing its path toward a NYSE listing under the ticker SECZ following a SPAC merger. The development represents a significant step toward bringing a major blockchain infrastructure company to traditional public markets.

crypto-web3.title: Satoshi-era bitcoin at center of $285 billion lawsuit moves after 14 years
crypto-web3.summary: A Bitcoin address dating to the Satoshi era holding potentially billions in value has moved for the first time in 14 years following a legal notice from Salomon Brothers demanding proof of ownership by November 5, 2025. The movement of the 1LwWt address has triggered significant speculation regarding its ownership and control.

crypto-web3.title: A massive hiring wave reveals trading firms are no longer viewing Polymarket as a niche betting tool
crypto-web3.summary: Quantitative trading firms are significantly expanding their presence in prediction market platforms Polymarket and Kalshi, with hiring surging to exploit market inefficiencies rather than simply trading event outcomes. The shift signals growing institutional recognition of prediction markets as sophisticated trading venues rather than niche betting platforms.

crypto-web3.title: Researcher who found Zcash's bug with AI adds Monero to his audit queue
crypto-web3.summary: Taylor Hornby, the security researcher who used artificial intelligence to uncover a critical vulnerability in Zcash's Orchard protocol that caused a 38% price decline, is expanding his AI-driven audit work to include other privacy coins. The development highlights how AI-powered security research is becoming increasingly important for identifying vulnerabilities across the crypto ecosystem.

crypto-web3.title: Hyperliquid treasuries stand alone in profit as legacy crypto DATs bleed billions
crypto-web3.summary: While Hyperliquid treasury strategies remain profitable, established treasury management firms including Strategy and Bitmine have experienced significant paper losses as cryptocurrency prices continue to decline. The divergence highlights shifting performance dynamics among digital asset treasury managers amid market volatility.

cybersecurity.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
cybersecurity.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and sensitive agency secrets on a public GitHub account, prompting Congressional demands for answers. The agency continues efforts to contain the breach and invalidate the exposed credentials.

cybersecurity.title: Miasma Worm Hits 73 Microsoft GitHub Repositories in Major Supply Chain Attack
cybersecurity.summary: A self-replicating supply chain attack campaign compromised 73 Microsoft repositories across four GitHub organizations including Azure and Microsoft, leading GitHub to disable access to the affected repositories. The incident demonstrates significant vulnerability in critical software development infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
cybersecurity.summary: Dutch authorities arrested two co-owners of internet hosting companies for operating infrastructure used by Russia to conduct cyberattacks, influence operations, and disinformation campaigns within the European Union. The seized servers were linked to sanctioned ISP Stark Industries Solutions.

cybersecurity.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager CVE-2026-20245 Flaw Actively Exploited – No Patch Available
cybersecurity.summary: Cisco has issued a warning regarding a high-severity vulnerability in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (CVSS 7.8) that is currently under active exploitation with no patch available. The flaw affects multiple deployment types including on-premise, cloud, and government systems.

cybersecurity.title: AI Agent Uncovers 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg; Chrome Patches Record 429 Bugs
cybersecurity.summary: An autonomous AI agent discovered 21 previously unknown vulnerabilities in FFmpeg, the widely-used media library embedded in video-related software, while Google simultaneously released Chrome 149 with a record 429 security patches. The findings underscore both emerging AI security capabilities and the scale of vulnerability challenges in critical software.

cybersecurity.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited SolarWinds Serv-U DoS Flaw to KEV Catalog
cybersecurity.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency added a high-severity denial-of-service vulnerability in SolarWinds Serv-U file server software to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog based on evidence of active exploitation. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-28318 with a CVSS score of 7.5, causes service crashes.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft released updates addressing 167 security vulnerabilities across Windows and related software, including a critical SharePoint Server zero-day and the publicly disclosed BlueHammer Windows Defender flaw. Additionally, Google Chrome patched its fourth zero-day of 2026, and Adobe issued emergency updates for an actively exploited Reader vulnerability enabling remote code execution.

cybersecurity.title: 'Scattered Spider' Member 'Tylerb' Pleads Guilty
cybersecurity.summary: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and identity theft for orchestrating text-message phishing attacks in 2022. The attacks compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in tens of millions of dollars stolen from cryptocurrency investors.

cybersecurity.title: Rust-Written IronWorm Hits NPM Supply Chain
cybersecurity.summary: A new supply chain attack campaign utilizing IronWorm targets developers to steal credentials, which are subsequently reused to propagate across the software supply chain. The attack mirrors tactics from similar campaigns and demonstrates persistent threats to the popular NPM package registry.

cybersecurity.title: Free Apps Are Quietly Turning Smart TVs Into Web-Scraping Proxies for AI
cybersecurity.summary: Security research revealed that Bright Data has embedded an iOS SDK in consumer applications that converts devices, including always-on smart TVs, into exit nodes for web-scraping traffic. The company operates what it claims is the world's largest residential proxy network, marketed heavily to the artificial intelligence industry.

startups-vc.title: Anthropic Funding Pushed Startup Investment To Near-Record Levels In May As Exit Market Reopened
startups-vc.summary: Global venture funding reached $92 billion in May, marking the second-largest monthly total on record, with Anthropic's $50 billion raise accounting for 54% of the month's total funding. The significant capital injection signals a reopening of the exit market and renewed investor confidence in the AI sector.

startups-vc.title: It Took Apple 42 Years to Reach $1 Trillion. Anthropic Will Do It in 5.
startups-vc.summary: Anthropic has raised $65 billion at a $965 billion valuation and filed confidentially for an IPO, demonstrating unprecedented scaling velocity in AI-driven company building compared to historical technology giants. The milestone illustrates how AI has fundamentally reset expectations around company growth timelines and valuations.

startups-vc.title: BillionToOne Goes Public — The Startup That Made Genetic Testing Universal
startups-vc.summary: BillionToOne became Y Combinator's fourth publicly traded biotech company following its IPO, with its fetal genetic test now used by 1 in 11 babies born in the United States. The company represents significant progress in democratizing access to genetic testing technology.

startups-vc.title: Meesho Goes Public
startups-vc.summary: Meesho, which launched in Y Combinator's S16 cohort, has gone public as one of India's largest e-commerce platforms, empowering millions of small merchants to scale their businesses. The IPO marks a decade of growth focused on user-centric product development in the Indian e-commerce market.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to Groww
startups-vc.summary: Groww, India's leading digital investing platform, has gone public, enabling tens of millions to invest in stocks, mutual funds, and ETFs through its platform. As Y Combinator's first company to list in India, the IPO represents a significant milestone for the accelerator's global portfolio.

startups-vc.title: How Vercel Runs on AI Agents: 96% of Marketing, 93% of Support, and an SDR Team Reabsorbed. A Deep Dive With CPO Tom Occhino
startups-vc.summary: Vercel has implemented AI agents across its operations, automating 96% of marketing and 93% of support functions, demonstrating how AI infrastructure can fundamentally transform organizational structure and efficiency. The deep dive with Chief Product Officer Tom Occhino provides insight into next-generation operational models in B2B SaaS.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to EquipmentShare on Going Public
startups-vc.summary: EquipmentShare, a Y Combinator W15 graduate, has gone public after expanding from Missouri to 373 locations nationwide with its contractor-focused operating system. The IPO validates the company's mission to build essential infrastructure for the construction industry.

startups-vc.title: Harshita Arora Joins YC as General Partner
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator announced Harshita Arora as a new General Partner, bringing deep fintech and infrastructure expertise along with a proven track record as a founder. The appointment strengthens YC's investment capabilities in critical infrastructure sectors.

startups-vc.title: SaaStr AI App of the Week: Pylon. The AI-Native Support Platform Built for B2B, Not Reheated B2C
startups-vc.summary: Pylon, an AI-native customer support platform purpose-built for B2B environments, has raised $51 million and is being adopted by leading AI-native companies including AssemblyAI and Cognition. The platform addresses the unique complexities of B2B support where conversations occur in Slack and Teams and accounts carry significant financial value.

startups-vc.title: 5 Interesting Startup Deals You May Have Missed: On-Demand Custom Manufacturing, Underwater Geothermal Energy, And Adventure Group Travel
startups-vc.summary: A diverse array of funded startups demonstrated varied investment opportunities, including a bootstrapped custom metal manufacturer securing its first outside Silicon Valley backing and a startup targeting geothermal energy generation from underwater volcanoes for small island nations. The deals highlight investor appetite for specialized manufacturing and renewable energy infrastructure solutions.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Lilly shares safety, tolerability data on its next-gen obesity drug
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly presented new safety and tolerability data on retatrutide, a next-generation triple hormone receptor agonist, at the American Diabetes Association annual meeting. The data advancement represents significant progress in the competitive obesity drug market, where retatrutide is positioned as a potential competitor to existing GLP-1 therapies.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Detailed data show Pfizer's monthly obesity drug continues to show potential
health-biotech.summary: Detailed midstage study results demonstrate that Pfizer's obesity drug acquired from biotech Metsera maintains efficacy with monthly dosing. The data supports the drug's potential as a convenient, longer-acting alternative in the expanding obesity treatment market.

health-biotech.title: Roche to start Phase 2 petrelintide, enicepatide combo trial this year
health-biotech.summary: Roche announced plans to initiate a Phase 2 combination trial of petrelintide and enicepatide this year, pursuing a dual-therapy approach to obesity with complementary mechanisms of action. The strategy aims to leverage the potential synergistic effects of combining drugs with distinct safety and efficacy profiles.

health-biotech.title: #ADA26: Pfizer monthly shot shows effect on obesity, diabetes
health-biotech.summary: Pfizer's long-acting GLP-1 candidate, Berobenatide, demonstrated clinical efficacy in patients with both obesity and type 2 diabetes in data presented at the American Diabetes Association conference. The results validate the acquisition strategy and support the drug's potential as a dual-indication therapy.

health-biotech.title: An ADC specialist sinks on safety concerns for blood cancer drug
health-biotech.summary: ADC Therapeutics experienced a sharp stock decline following Phase 3 trial results showing a death rate in a blood cancer drug that may be difficult for physicians and regulators to accept. The development highlights ongoing safety challenges in antibody-drug conjugate development.

health-biotech.title: Generic drugmakers gain key victory in 'skinny label' patent case
health-biotech.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a generic version of Amarin's Vascepa did not infringe on the company's patents, a decision with significant implications for generic drug availability and patent protection strategies. The ruling reshapes the landscape for future generic competition in specialty pharmaceuticals.

health-biotech.title: Otsuka showcases kidney drug data; Pfizer cuts an AI deal
health-biotech.summary: Otsuka presented data on Voyxact demonstrating kidney function preservation benefits, though results left openings for competing IgAN therapies from Vera and Vertex. The data release underscores the intensifying competition in rare kidney disease treatments.

health-biotech.title: Alnylam, Inceptive ink AI deal potentially worth $2B
health-biotech.summary: Alnylam partnered with AI platform Inceptive in a deal worth up to $2 billion to enhance RNA drug discovery and development prioritization. The collaboration leverages artificial intelligence to accelerate the research process and identify optimal development candidates in RNA therapeutics.

health-biotech.title: Pharma is betting big on PD-1/VEGF bispecifics. But are companies chasing the wrong target?
health-biotech.summary: While major pharmaceutical companies focus on developing PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibodies for lung cancer treatment, emerging evidence suggests the liver may represent a more promising therapeutic target. The analysis questions the current industry focus and highlights potential resource misallocation.

health-biotech.title: Opinion: I led the U.S. CDC response to the 2014 Ebola epidemic. The new outbreak needs massive, immediate, meticulous action
health-biotech.summary: Tom Frieden, former CDC director of the 2014-2016 Ebola outbreak response, urges immediate and comprehensive action on the current Ebola outbreak, emphasizing the disease's unforgiving nature and the need for rapid, coordinated global response. His commentary reflects critical lessons from prior epidemic management.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: China's CO2 climbs 2% in early 2026 due to 'wasted' wind and solar
energy-climate.summary: China's carbon dioxide emissions increased by 2% in the first quarter of 2026, revealing a critical infrastructure challenge where renewable energy generation exceeds grid capacity to distribute and utilize it. The analysis highlights that despite massive renewable energy expansion, transmission constraints and grid management issues are undermining decarbonization efforts.

energy-climate.title: Critical minerals are becoming the energy transition's delivery constraint
energy-climate.summary: Critical minerals have emerged as a defining bottleneck for the renewable energy sector as it enters a more complex industrial phase of expansion. The shortage of essential materials threatens to slow the pace of global energy transition implementation across multiple technologies.

energy-climate.title: Q&A: How UK's seventh carbon budget will deliver '£865bn' in economic benefits
energy-climate.summary: The UK Labour government aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions to 87% below 1990 levels through its seventh carbon budget framework, with analysis projecting substantial economic benefits. The policy demonstrates the potential for ambitious climate targets to generate broader economic gains across the British economy.

energy-climate.title: Global grid capex to surpass $650 billion in 2026, says Rystad Energy
energy-climate.summary: Global capital expenditure on grid infrastructure is projected to exceed $650 billion in 2026, supported by transformer manufacturing capacity reaching 4,700 gigavolt-amperes across more than 400 plants worldwide. The substantial investment underscores the energy market's critical dependence on securing adequate grid equipment to support renewable energy integration.

energy-climate.title: Age of electrification exposing Australia's weakest link
energy-climate.summary: Australia's renewable energy penetration in its National Electricity Market has surged from 15% in 2015 to 46.5% in 2026, yet the rapid electrification is exposing critical infrastructure vulnerabilities. The dramatic renewable expansion is outpacing grid modernization efforts, creating reliability and integration challenges.

energy-climate.title: Trump administration announces $850M to modernize US coal capacity, build 2 new plants
energy-climate.summary: The Trump administration is allocating $850 million to modernize coal capacity and construct two new coal-fired plants totaling 2.85 GW in Alaska and West Virginia, marking the first new U.S. coal facilities since 2013. The move represents a significant reversal of energy policy direction amid the global renewable energy transition.

energy-climate.title: ContourGlobal launches Victor Jara hybrid plant in Chile
energy-climate.summary: ContourGlobal has commenced operations at the Victor Jara hybrid power plant in Chile, featuring a 231-megawatt solar installation paired with 1.3 gigawatt-hours of battery storage. The project demonstrates the growing deployment of integrated renewable and storage solutions in Latin America.

energy-climate.title: Fervo Energy faces transmission constraints in the West, analysts say
energy-climate.summary: Enhanced geothermal developer Fervo Energy is encountering significant transmission constraints in western U.S. markets, prompting consideration of behind-the-meter solutions as alternative deployment strategies. The challenge reflects broader grid infrastructure limitations affecting renewable and emerging clean energy technologies.

energy-climate.title: EDF and Masdar sign 15-year PPAs for BigBeau Solar+Storage output
energy-climate.summary: EDF Power Solutions North America and the Abu Dhabi-based Masdar have executed 15-year power purchase agreements for the BigBeau Solar+Storage project in California's Kern County. The long-term contracts provide revenue stability for the integrated solar and storage facility.

energy-climate.title: Prysmian completes cable installation for Sofia offshore wind farm
energy-climate.summary: Prysmian Group has concluded its subsea cable installation contract for RWE's 1.4-gigawatt Sofia offshore wind farm located on Dogger Bank in the North Sea. The project completion represents a significant milestone in expanding offshore wind capacity in northern European waters.

asia-pacific.title: Rookie Taiwan lawmaker blacklisted by Beijing makes long-shot bid to become Taipei mayor
asia-pacific.summary:
A first-term lawmaker who was blacklisted by Beijing over his alleged support for Taiwanese independence will challenge Taipei’s popular incumbent mayor in the local elections later this year.
The ruling Democratic Progressive Party’s decision to nominate Puma Shen Pao-yang, an outspoken critic of Beijing whose political identity has been built around his calls to strengthen the island’s security, came as a surprise to many.
The former academic joined politics only two years ago, entering the...

asia-pacific.title: Indonesia’s labour movement is deeply divided over political patronage
asia-pacific.summary:
In Jakarta last month, many May Day demonstrations took place, but two stood out as reflections of the divisions among Indonesia’s trade unions.
At the National Monument (Monas), tens of thousands of workers gathered at a rally with President Prabowo Subianto as the guest of honour. Several kilometres away at the People’s Representative Council complex, around 10,000 people assembled under a different banner: “May Day with the People”. Their stated position was an explicit refusal to be co-opted

asia-pacific.title: Malaysian hiker missing for 2 weeks miraculously survives on forest berries
asia-pacific.summary:
Jaslinda Saludin, the Malaysian hiker who went missing after descending Gunung Batu Putih mountain in Perak state’s Tapah two weeks ago, has been found and is now undergoing treatment.
“Thank you to everyone involved, especially the police, Fire and Rescue Department personnel, NGOs, the Orang Asli (indigenous people) and all those who participated in the search operation,” said her husband, Haszman Othman, 61, who was full of gratitude over the successful rescue.
He was met by the media at the.

asia-pacific.title: Thaw in China-India ties is real, but don’t call it reconciliation
asia-pacific.summary:
Six years after the Ladakh crisis erupted in May 2020, triggering the worst military confrontation between India and China in decades, the two countries are attempting something that once seemed improbable: a diplomatic reset.
The latest indication came late last month when officials met in Beijing for the 35th meeting of the Working Mechanism for Consultation and Coordination on India-China Border Affairs. The message emerging from the talks was positive. The two sides reaffirmed their...

asia-pacific.title: How travel permit for non-Chinese in Hong Kong has redefined weekends over border
asia-pacific.summary:
Visiting indoor ski resorts, enjoying water parks and eating at dim sum restaurants in Shenzhen and other nearby cities have become weekend draws for Hong Kong residents holding foreign passports, who praise a travel permit that has made crossing the border easier and faster.
But even two years after the launch of the scheme, some hiccups remain, such as the lack of recognition of the permit by some mainland Chinese immigration staff, representatives of non-Chinese residents and foreign business

asia-pacific.title: China grandma, 74, gains online fame for fashion sense, youthful spirit, inspires with confidence
asia-pacific.summary:
A “girlish grandma” from eastern China is taking the internet by storm with her dazzling white hair, colourful outfits and fearless confidence.
Yingzi, 74, from Shanghai, has more than two million online followers and often dances or poses on city streets in heels.
Her videos have reportedly racked up over 100 million views at their peak.
On her social media, Yingzi shares the secrets behind her youthful figure.
She says it starts with “sticking to your style,” choosing clothes that young people

asia-pacific.title: Malaysia’s gas-guzzling data centre boom clashes with its clean energy goals
asia-pacific.summary:
Malaysia has staked its economic future on becoming Southeast Asia’s data-centre capital. It has also promised to slash fossil fuel use by 2050. Right now, those two ambitions are pulling in opposite directions – and gas is winning.
There were 54 operational data centres across Malaysia by the end of 2024, with that number expected to rise to 81 by 2035, government minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir told parliament last year.
In the handful of years from 2021 and mid-2025, some 144.4 billion...

asia-pacific.title: Multiple people shot near festival in Toledo, Ohio
asia-pacific.summary:
Multiple people were shot on Saturday near a busy community street festival in Toledo, Ohio, and a search for the suspects was ongoing as victims were taken to nearby hospitals, police said.
Toledo police officers responded to a report of a person shot near the Old West End Festival at about 5.30pm. When they arrived, they found multiple shooting victims, the police department said in a statement.
Police said “many victims” were transported to medical facilities but did not provide further...

asia-pacific.title: Norman Bethune’s story still holds lessons for China-Canada relations
asia-pacific.summary:
I grew up in a classroom where the name Norman Bethune was invoked with reverence. Like every schoolchild in China, I could recite from memory Chairman Mao’s 1939 essay “In Memory of Norman Bethune”, which characterised Bethune as a man who had come from afar, who gave his life to the Chinese revolution, who embodied selflessness and internationalism.
For years, I kept a poster in my office – the famous oil painting of Mao meeting Bethune in Yan’an – as a quiet tribute. As the years passed and..

asia-pacific.title: Missing US student found dead in Japan
asia-pacific.summary:
An ⁠American student ⁠who disappeared while on ⁠a family holiday in Japan was found dead outside Kyoto, his mother wrote in a social media post on Saturday.
The body of James ‌Higginbotham, 20, was discovered in a mountainous area by a volunteer search-and-rescue group, Nancy Higginbotham wrote.
A cause of death and further details were not immediately available.
“Our family is heartbroken,” she said. “The grief we feel is ⁠impossible to put into words.”
Higginbotham, an Alabama resident ‌and an
